• 2025 Oct Chair (with Shreya Saxena, Guillaume Hennequin)
    Champalimaud Research Symposium 2025 — Neuro-Cybernetics at Scale
    https://neurocybernetics.cc
  • 2025 Apr Co-Organizer (with Sool Park)
    Beyond Clarity: The first smooth exchange between the discrete and the continuous
    https://beyond-clarity.github.io/
  • 2024 Mar Workshop Co-organizer (with Daniel Durstewitz)
    COSYNE 2024 workshop — Reconstructing Dynamical Systems from Neural Data
    https://sites.google.com/research.fchampalimaud.org/cosyne-dynamical-systems-ws
  • 2023 Oct Planning committee
    Time Research Forum 3
    https://timingforum.org/
  • 2022 Oct Organizer
    Neural Latent State and Dynamics Inference Workshop
    https://github.com/catniplab/latent_dynamics_workshop/
  • 2022 Apr – 2023 Jul Co-Director
    Cajal course on Machine Learning for Neuroscience
    https://cajal-training.org/on-site/artificial-intelligence/
  • 2020 Apr Co-organizer (with Jeffrey Heinz, Christian Luhmann, Heather Lynch)
    Interpretable AI: Across the Disciplines
    https://interpretable2020.wordpress.com/
    (cancelled due to COVID-19 pandemic)
  • 2019 Oct Local Organizing Committee Member
    Deepmath
    https://www.deepmath-conference.com/
  • 2019 Mar Workshop Co-organizer (with Benjamin F. Grewe et al.)
    COSYNE 2019 workshop — Why Spike?
    https://whyspike.wordpress.com/
  • 2016 Jul Workshop Co-organizer (with Ian Stevenson)
    Organization for Computational Neuroscience (CNS) 2016 workshop — Statistical analysis of neural time series
    http://stevensonlab.github.io/cns2016-neural-timeseries/
  • 2014 Dec Workshop Co-organizer (with Ferran Diego, Jeremy Freeman, Jakob Macke, Eftychios Pnevmatikakis)
    NIPS 2014 workshop — Large scale optical physiology: From data-acquisition to models of neural coding
    http://hci.iwr.uni-heidelberg.de/Staff/fdiego/LargeScaleOpticalPhysiology/
  • 2014 Mar Workshop Co-organizer (with Evan Archer, Jonathan Pillow)
    Computational Systems Neuroscience (COSYNE) 2014 workshop — Scalable models for high-dimensional neural data
    http://scalablemodels.wordpress.com/
